sourceMovie.title: Locke
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Locke works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Unfaithful
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Locke clicked for you, Unfaithful (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Locke clicked for you, Derailed (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Locke?
faq.answer: Try Unfaithful, Derailed, Black Bear—each shares DNA with Locke while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Locke?
faq.answer: Locke (2014) is best described as Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Locke worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
